output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back in 60 Seconds
output.meta_description: Landlord withholding your security deposit? Know your rights and send a legally sound demand letter in 60 seconds for just $19.99—no signup needed.
content_outline: Understanding the Deadline: How Long Your Landlord Really Has to Return Your Deposit
Illegal Deductions: What Your Landlord Can and Cannot Charge You For
The 60-Second Solution: Sending a Formal Demand Letter Without a Lawyer
key_points: Most US states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 60 days after move-out.
Landlords cannot deduct for normal wear and tear; only actual damage beyond ordinary use or unpaid rent.
Sending a formal letter of demand is the fastest, most effective first step before small claims court.
Deposits-Back.com generates a state-specific security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for a flat $19.99 with no signup or subscription.
output.cta: Stop waiting. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds for just $19.99—no subscription, no signup required.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters dealing with a landlord who is withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting their security deposit.

social_hooks.hook: POV: It's been 45 days since move-out and your landlord finally replies... 'I'm keeping it for cleaning.'
Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? They're betting you won't do this one thing.
My landlord said I wouldn't get my deposit back because I broke the lease. Then I sent THIS letter.
What to do when your landlord doesn't return your deposit: stop begging and start demanding.
Landlord not returning your deposit? This $19.99 letter got me my $1,800 back in 10 days.
